project.name: fyp_quant
project.description: Research benchmarking framework studying safety-capability trade-offs in quantized small language models (1.7-7.2B, five matched pairs): fp16 baseline vs on-the-fly NF4 4-bit / INT8 loading across HarmBench (ASR, judge-scored), XSTest (over-refusal), and MMLU (accuracy), with statistical pairwise analysis, a Streamlit dashboard, TC1 SLURM tooling, generated report/thesis deliverables, and a self-verifying agent harness.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the benchmark plugin layer, re-exporting the four benchmark plugins (ARC, HarmBench, MMLU, XSTest) plus the BenchmarkItem/BenchmarkPlugin base types.

nodes.name: arc.py
nodes.summary: ARC-Challenge benchmark plugin measuring multiple-choice science reasoning accuracy; loads the allenai/ai2_arc dataset, builds lettered MC prompts, and scores via exact choice-index match.

nodes.name: ARCConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding ARC dataset settings: dataset name, config name (ARC-Challenge), and split.

nodes.name: ARCChallengePlugin
nodes.summary: BenchmarkPlugin implementation for ARC-Challenge: loads and shuffles dataset rows into BenchmarkItems, scores responses with the shared choice-index parser, and aggregates overall plus per-subject accuracy.

nodes.name: _gold_index_from_label
nodes.summary: Resolves an ARC answer key (letter, numeric string, or exact label match) to a zero-based gold choice index, returning None when unresolvable.

nodes.name: _build_mc_prompt
nodes.summary: Formats an ARC question and its choices into a lettered (A/B/C/...) multiple-choice prompt instructing the model to answer with a single letter.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the benchmark plugin contract: the frozen BenchmarkItem prompt dataclass and the BenchmarkPlugin ABC (load_items, build_prompt, score_response, aggregate) that every benchmark implements.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python's abc.ABC with @abstractmethod plus a frozen dataclass for immutable prompt items.

nodes.name: BenchmarkItem
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing a single benchmark prompt: stable prompt_id, prompt_text, and a metadata dict for benchmark-specific fields.

nodes.name: BenchmarkPlugin
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the four-method contract (load_items, build_prompt, score_response, aggregate) that HarmBench, XSTest, MMLU, and ARC plugins all implement.

nodes.name: harmbench.py
nodes.summary: HarmBench benchmark plugin measuring harmful compliance: loads walledai/HarmBench prompts and scores responses with the v2 refusal/malformed detectors to produce the (proxy) attack success rate; the official HarmBench classifier judge is the primary ASR scorer downstream.

nodes.name: HarmBenchConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass with HarmBench dataset settings (name, split, config) plus optional extra refusal patterns merged into the default detector.

nodes.name: HarmBenchPlugin
nodes.summary: BenchmarkPlugin for HarmBench: loads harmful prompts (with a config-name fallback), classifies each response as refusal/malformed/attack-success via shared utils, and aggregates attack_success_rate, refusal_rate, and malformed_rate.

nodes.name: _extract_prompt_text
nodes.summary: Pulls the harmful prompt string from a HarmBench dataset row by trying several candidate column names (prompt, behavior, etc.) and returning the first non-empty string.

nodes.name: mmlu.py
nodes.summary: MMLU benchmark plugin measuring general capability: loads a cais/mmlu subject subset, builds lettered multiple-choice prompts, scores by exact choice-index match, and reports overall plus per-subject accuracy.

nodes.name: MMLUConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass with MMLU dataset settings: dataset name, split, and the subject (config) selection.

nodes.name: MMLUPlugin
nodes.summary: BenchmarkPlugin for MMLU: loads subject rows into BenchmarkItems with gold indices, scores model responses via the shared choice-index parser, and aggregates accuracy overall and by subject.

nodes.name: _parse_gold_index
nodes.summary: Normalises an MMLU answer field (int index, digit string, or letter) into a validated zero-based gold choice index, returning None when out of range or unparseable.

nodes.name: _build_mc_prompt
nodes.summary: Formats an MMLU question and choices into a lettered multiple-choice prompt asking the model to reply with a single answer letter.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Factory registry mapping a benchmark name string (harmbench, xstest, mmlu, arc) to a constructed plugin instance; the pipeline's single entry point for instantiating benchmark plugins.

nodes.name: build_benchmark_plugin
nodes.summary: Normalises the benchmark name and dispatches to the matching plugin constructor (HarmBenchPlugin, XSTestPlugin, MMLUPlugin, ARCChallengePlugin), raising ValueError for unknown names.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared scoring utilities for all benchmark plugins: the v2 deterministic refusal-pattern detector (DEFAULT_REFUSAL_PATTERNS plus refusal-verb regexes), malformed-response detection, seeded shuffle-and-limit sampling, and the multiple-choice answer-index parser.
nodes.languageNotes: Refusal patterns are matched after lower-casing and normalising curly apostrophes/quotes to ASCII, so one straight-quote pattern covers both forms.

nodes.name: shuffle_and_limit
nodes.summary: Deterministically shuffles benchmark items with a seeded random.Random and truncates to max_samples, guaranteeing reproducible sample selection across runs.

nodes.name: is_malformed_response
nodes.summary: Flags degenerate model outputs (empty, too short, or with too low an alphabetic/alphanumeric character ratio) so capability collapse is not miscounted as compliance or refusal.

nodes.name: _normalise_for_refusal
nodes.summary: Lower-cases response text and replaces curly apostrophes/quotation marks with straight ASCII equivalents before refusal-pattern matching.

nodes.name: match_refusal_pattern
nodes.summary: Runs the normalised response against DEFAULT_REFUSAL_PATTERNS (plus any extra config patterns) and returns the first matching pattern string, or None; the core of the v2 refusal classifier.

nodes.name: is_refusal_response
nodes.summary: Boolean wrapper over match_refusal_pattern used by HarmBench and XSTest scoring to decide whether a response is a refusal.

nodes.name: parse_choice_index
nodes.summary: Extracts the model's chosen multiple-choice index from free-form text using a priority cascade: leading letter, explicit answer declarations, last in-range letter mention, then a bare-number fallback.

nodes.name: xstest.py
nodes.summary: XSTest benchmark plugin measuring over-refusal on benign prompts: loads the local xstest_v2_prompts.csv (or HF fallback), filters to benign prompts via type/label heuristics, and scores refusals with the shared v2 detector to compute over_refusal_rate.

nodes.name: XSTestConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass with XSTest source settings: local CSV path, HF dataset name/split/config fallback, and the benign_only filter flag.

nodes.name: XSTestPlugin
nodes.summary: BenchmarkPlugin for XSTest: prefers the committed local CSV over HF loading, keeps benign prompts (warning when unsafe/unlabelled rows are dropped), scores refusal/malformed per response, and aggregates over_refusal_rate over benign items.

nodes.name: _extract_prompt_text
nodes.summary: Pulls the prompt string from an XSTest row by trying several candidate column names and returning the first non-empty string.

nodes.name: _classify_benign
nodes.summary: Tri-state benign classifier for an XSTest row: inspects type/label/category fields (contrast_* types are unsafe, plain categories safe by convention), explicit safe/unsafe labels, and contrast cues in the id, returning True/False/None.

nodes.name: _is_benign_prompt
nodes.summary: Boolean wrapper over _classify_benign that defaults unrecognised rows to benign (True) for backwards-compatible filtering.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the legacy dataset loaders and sample dataclasses for toxicity (RealToxicityPrompts), bias (BBQ), and factuality (TruthfulQA) tasks.

nodes.name: rescore_harmbench.py
nodes.summary: Post-hoc rescoring script that re-applies the corrected v2 refusal classifier to existing HarmBench and XSTest raw.jsonl results and writes derived scores.v2.jsonl / summary.v2.json sidecars plus redacted diagnostics, without touching the immutable TC1-original artifacts or re-running inference.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately never writes raw response text to outputs: diagnostics carry only IDs, boolean flags, matched pattern indices, and response lengths.

nodes.name: _rescore_harmbench_record
nodes.summary: Recomputes a single HarmBench record's score_fields (refusal, malformed, attack_success) with the updated classifier and returns the corrected fields plus the matched pattern.

nodes.name: _rescore_xstest_record
nodes.summary: Recomputes a single XSTest record's score_fields with the updated refusal classifier, preserving the benign flag and deriving the over_refusal indicator.

nodes.name: _aggregate_harmbench
nodes.summary: Aggregates rescored HarmBench records into corrected attack_success_rate, refusal_rate, and malformed_rate metrics for summary.v2.json.

nodes.name: _aggregate_xstest
nodes.summary: Aggregates rescored XSTest records into corrected over_refusal_rate and related benign-subset metrics for summary.v2.json.

nodes.name: _score_sidecar_row
nodes.summary: Builds one redacted scores.v2.jsonl row containing the prompt_id and corrected score_fields only, deliberately excluding prompt and response text.

nodes.name: _rescore_benchmark
nodes.summary: Core per-(model, benchmark) rescoring pass: reads raw.jsonl, recomputes score fields for every record, tracks before/after flag flips into diagnostic rows, and writes the v2 sidecar files unless in dry-run mode.

nodes.name: rescore_model
nodes.summary: Runs _rescore_benchmark for both harmbench and xstest for a single model alias, collecting the combined change summary.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: discovers model result directories, rescoring each with optional dry-run, then writes the redacted rescore_diagnostics CSV and rescore_aggregate.json summary under results/analysis.

nodes.name: test_arc.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the ARC-Challenge plugin: answer-key resolution, dataset-schema parsing into BenchmarkItems, exact-match scoring, accuracy aggregation, and registry construction.

nodes.name: test_load_items_parses_arc_schema
nodes.summary: Verifies ARCChallengePlugin.load_items converts a mocked ai2_arc row (question, choices, answerKey) into a BenchmarkItem with the correct prompt text and gold index metadata.

nodes.name: test_aggregate_accuracy
nodes.summary: Checks that ARCChallengePlugin.aggregate computes overall and per-subject accuracy correctly from scored items.

nodes.name: test_refusal_parser.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the shared v2 scoring utilities: refusal-pattern positives/negatives (including curly-apostrophe normalisation), malformed-response detection, and the choice-index parser's priority cascade and edge cases.

nodes.name: test_refusal_parser_handles_curly_apostrophes
nodes.summary: Asserts refusals written with Unicode curly apostrophes are still detected, exercising the text normalisation step of the v2 classifier.

nodes.name: test_parse_choice_answer_declaration_recovers_verbose
nodes.summary: Verifies parse_choice_index recovers the intended letter from verbose responses containing an explicit answer declaration rather than a bare leading letter.

nodes.name: test_parse_choice_markdown_emphasis_recovered_by_intentional_rule
nodes.summary: Checks that answers wrapped in markdown emphasis (e.g. **B**) are recovered by the parser's intentional-declaration rule instead of being missed.

nodes.name: test_xstest_benign.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for XSTest benign-prompt classification: contrast types are unsafe, plain categories are safe by convention, explicit labels and id cues are honoured, and unrecognised rows default to benign.

nodes.name: check_slurm_runs.py
nodes.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM run status checker's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.check_runs.

nodes.name: check_runs.py
nodes.summary: SLURM run status checker: cross-references expected (model, benchmark) result paths from the quantization config against completed summary.json files, submitted-job manifests, and live squeue output to report completed, running, and missing runs.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI for the status checker: config path, results dir, jobs dir, output path, squeue skip flag, and log level.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging with a level parsed from the CLI string argument.

nodes.name: _load_submissions
nodes.summary: Reads submitted_jobs.json from the jobs directory (if present) to recover job-name to SLURM-job-id mappings recorded at submission time.

nodes.name: _current_squeue_job_ids
nodes.summary: Polls squeue via subprocess for the current user and returns the set of active SLURM job ids, tolerating environments where squeue is unavailable.

nodes.name: check_status
nodes.summary: Core status routine: loads the validated quantization config, enumerates every expected model x benchmark run, and classifies each as completed (summary.json exists), running (job id still in squeue), or missing, returning an aggregate report dict.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint: parses args, runs check_status, optionally writes the JSON status report to disk, and logs a summary line.

nodes.name: generate_jobs.py
nodes.summary: Generates per-model or per-(model,benchmark) SLURM sbatch scripts from the validated quantization config, embedding the TC1 bootstrap prologue (work_dir cd, setup_commands, log dir mkdir) and writing a manifest.json used by the submitter.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses shlex.quote throughout when assembling shell command lines, and supports group_by=model to emit matrix jobs that load each model once.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI for job generation: config, results/jobs dirs, python binary, runner script, seed, device, max samples, and group-by mode.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging with a level parsed from the CLI string argument.

nodes.name: _sbatch_text
nodes.summary: Renders the full sbatch script text: SBATCH directives from SlurmConfig (partition, qos, gpus, mem, time) plus a prologue that cds into work_dir, mkdirs the log dir, and runs setup_commands before the benchmark command.

nodes.name: generate_job_scripts
nodes.summary: Main generation routine: for each configured model (grouped by model for matrix jobs, or per model-benchmark pair), builds the runner command line, writes an sbatch file into jobs_dir, and records every job in manifest.json.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint: parses args, loads and validates the quantization config, and calls generate_job_scripts, logging how many sbatch files were written.

nodes.name: submit_jobs.py
nodes.summary: Submits generated sbatch scripts to SLURM: reads manifest.json from the jobs directory, runs sbatch for each entry (or logs in dry-run mode), parses returned job ids, and persists submissions to submitted_jobs.json.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI for job submission: jobs directory, dry-run flag, and log level.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging with a level parsed from the CLI string argument.

nodes.name: load_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads and returns the manifest.json job list written by generate_jobs, raising if the manifest is absent from the jobs directory.

nodes.name: submit_all
nodes.summary: Iterates the manifest and invokes sbatch per job via subprocess, extracting the SLURM job id with a regex, recording success/failure per submission, and writing submitted_jobs.json; dry-run mode records without submitting.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entrypoint: parses args, calls submit_all on the jobs directory, and logs how many submissions succeeded.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the quant module re-exporting QuantizationConfig and load_quant_config from config_schema.

nodes.name: config_schema.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema layer for the whole study: defines decoding, model-entry, benchmark, and SLURM config models plus cross-validation (every pair_id needs a baseline and a quantized member; model benchmark lists must reference declared benchmarks) and the load_quant_config YAML loader.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic field/model validators extensively, including a model-level cross-validation pass that enforces pair completeness and quant_method/quantized consistency.

nodes.name: DecodingSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for decoding controls: max_new_tokens, temperature, top_p, repetition_penalty, max_input_tokens, and chat-template usage.

nodes.name: ModelEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one model alias: family, size, pair_id, HF model_id, benchmark list, dtype, attn_implementation, and quantization flags, with validators normalizing/rejecting bad values and enforcing quant_method consistency with the quantized flag.

nodes.name: BenchmarkEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one benchmark's dataset settings: dataset name/split/config, optional local CSV, sample and batch limits, MMLU subjects, refusal patterns, and benign-only filtering.

nodes.name: SlurmConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for SLURM job parameters (partition, qos, gpus, cpus, mem, walltime, log/work dirs, setup_commands) with a regex validator that rejects bare-minute time strings in favor of colon forms.

nodes.name: QuantizationConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level study config aggregating models, benchmarks, decoding, and SLURM sections; cross-validates that model benchmark lists reference declared benchmark keys and that every pair_id has at least one baseline and one quantized member.

nodes.name: load_quant_config
nodes.summary: Loads a YAML config file with yaml.safe_load, rejects non-mapping documents with a synthesized ValidationError, and returns a validated QuantizationConfig.

nodes.name: fyp_cli.py
nodes.summary: Unified project CLI with subcommands for smoke runs, single benchmark runs, the full model x benchmark matrix, pairwise analysis, SLURM cluster generate/submit/check, and agent-harness status/start packets, dispatching into the pipeline, analysis, cluster, and harness modules.

nodes.name: _add_common_options
nodes.summary: Attaches the shared --config, --log-level, and results-dir options (optionally with defaults) to a parser or subparser.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the full subcommand tree (smoke, run, matrix, analyze, cluster-generate, cluster-submit, cluster-check, agent-status, agent-start) with per-command arguments and defaults.

nodes.name: _run_analysis
nodes.summary: Runs the pairwise analysis pipeline: builds the pairwise delta report, pair-label summary, scale sensitivity, and cross-family consistency, then writes the JSON and CSV outputs into the analysis directory.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Command dispatcher: routes each subcommand to execute_quant_benchmark, run_quant_matrix, _run_analysis, generate_job_scripts, submit_all, check_status, or the agent-harness status/start-packet builders.

nodes.name: generate_slurm_jobs.py
nodes.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM job generator's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.generate_jobs.

nodes.name: submit_slurm_jobs.py
nodes.summary: Root-level convenience entrypoint that simply invokes the SLURM submission helper's main() from ethical_benchmark.cluster.submit_jobs.

nodes.name: test_quant_config_schema.py
nodes.summary: Tests locking down the live config shape (10 models, 5 pairs, 4 families; Phi eager attention, Mistral defaults, shared methodology across new pairs) and the ModelEntry attn_implementation validator.

nodes.name: TestLiveConfigShape
nodes.summary: Asserts invariants of the committed default config: ten models forming five baseline/4-bit pairs across four families, Phi/Mistral flag settings, methodology parity for the T26 pairs, and unchanged legacy models.

nodes.name: TestAttnImplementationField
nodes.summary: Unit tests for ModelEntry.attn_implementation: defaults to None, accepts and case-normalizes 'eager', and rejects typos with a validation error.

nodes.name: test_sensitivity.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the decoding-sensitivity study tooling: validates the sensitivity config is stochastic and schema-valid, checks delta-summary stability math, and verifies the sensitivity sbatch generator and v2-summary-preferring ASR reader loaded dynamically from scripts/.
nodes.languageNotes: Loads standalone scripts as modules via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location rather than package imports.

nodes.name: test_sensitivity_config_is_valid_and_stochastic
nodes.summary: Validates the sensitivity YAML config through load_quant_config and asserts it uses stochastic decoding settings distinct from the primary greedy study.

nodes.name: test_summarise_deltas_stability
nodes.summary: Exercises the sensitivity script's summarise_deltas over synthetic seed deltas to confirm stability classification behaves as expected.

nodes.name: test_generator_emits_expected_files
nodes.summary: Checks the sensitivity sbatch generator writes the expected set of job files, including the judge sbatch, into a temp directory.

nodes.name: test_v2_asr_prefers_v2_summary
nodes.summary: Asserts the sensitivity analyzer reads ASR from summary.v2.json when both it and the runtime summary.json exist.

nodes.name: test_slurm_helpers.py
nodes.summary: Tests for SLURM job generation and submission: job counts per grouping mode, matrix-runner reuse under group_by=model, dry-run submission with submitted_jobs.json output, work_dir/setup_commands/log-dir prologue content, relative script resolution, and walltime format validation.

nodes.name: test_generate_jobs_count
nodes.summary: Verifies generate_job_scripts writes one sbatch per model-benchmark pair plus a manifest.json in the default grouping mode.

nodes.name: test_generate_jobs_group_by_model_reuses_matrix_runner
nodes.summary: Asserts group_by=model emits one sbatch per model that invokes the matrix runner (loading the model once for all its benchmarks).

nodes.name: test_submit_all_dry_run
nodes.summary: Runs submit_all in dry-run mode over generated jobs and checks all submissions are recorded without invoking sbatch, with submitted_jobs.json written.

nodes.name: test_generate_jobs_include_workdir_and_setup_commands
nodes.summary: Checks the generated sbatch text contains the cd-into-work_dir line and every configured setup command in the prologue.

nodes.name: test_generate_jobs_resolves_relative_script_under_workdir
nodes.summary: Asserts a relative runner script path is resolved under the configured SLURM work_dir in the generated command line.

nodes.name: test_generate_jobs_includes_log_dir_mkdir
nodes.summary: Checks the sbatch prologue mkdirs the log directory so SLURM output/error paths exist before the job writes to them.

nodes.name: config_schema.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schema for the legacy ethical-benchmark YAML config (models, decoding, toxicity/bias/factuality tasks), validating configuration before any model download or dataset fetch begins. Distinct from the current quantization study's schema in ethical_benchmark/quant/config_schema.py.

nodes.name: ModelEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model describing one HuggingFace model entry (hf_id, trust_remote_code, dtype, revision) with a validator restricting dtype to an allowed set.

nodes.name: DecodingSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for generation decoding parameters: max_new_tokens, temperature, top_p, repetition_penalty, max_input_tokens, and chat-template usage.

nodes.name: ToxicityEvaluationSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for toxicity evaluation settings (threshold, classifier backend/model/batch size, bootstrap resamples) with a validator restricting classifier_type to supported backends.

nodes.name: BiasEvaluationSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for bias evaluation settings: the answer-letter regex pattern and bootstrap resample count.

nodes.name: FactualityEvaluationSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for factuality evaluation settings (optional LLM judge, judge scale min/max) with a validator ensuring the scale ordering is consistent.

nodes.name: TaskSchema
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one benchmark task: dataset name/split/config, sample cap, batch size, and the nested evaluation settings block.

nodes.name: BenchmarkConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level Pydantic config aggregating models, decoding parameters, and the task map for a legacy benchmark run.

nodes.name: validate_config
nodes.summary: Validates a raw config dict against BenchmarkConfig via model_validate, surfacing schema errors before pipeline execution.

nodes.name: bias.py
nodes.summary: Dataset loader for BBQ (Bias Benchmark for QA): loads and shuffles the HF dataset, robustly extracts multiple-choice options and gold/stereotyped indices from heterogeneous row formats, and builds lettered MC prompts as immutable BiasSample records.
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive parsing handles multiple BBQ export formats (ans0/1/2 keys, choices lists, letter or integer answer encodings).

nodes.name: BiasSample
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing one BBQ item: prompt, question, choices, gold index, bias axis, and optional stereotyped-answer index.

nodes.name: load_bbq
nodes.summary: Loads the BBQ dataset from HuggingFace, validates inputs, shuffles with a seed, caps sample count, and converts rows into BiasSample objects while skipping malformed entries.

nodes.name: _extract_choices
nodes.summary: Extracts the answer-choice list from a BBQ row, supporting ans0/ans1/ans2 keys, a choices list, or an answers list.

nodes.name: _parse_choice_index
nodes.summary: Parses a gold-answer value that may be an int, digit string, or letter (A/B/C) into a validated zero-based choice index, returning None when out of range or unparseable.

nodes.name: _build_bbq_prompt
nodes.summary: Formats context, question, and lettered choices into the final multiple-choice prompt string presented to the model.

nodes.name: factuality.py
nodes.summary: Dataset loader for TruthfulQA factuality benchmarking: loads the HF dataset, extracts multiple-choice targets from mc1 payloads or flat choices/labels arrays, and emits lettered MC prompts as FactualitySample records.

nodes.name: FactualitySample
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass for one TruthfulQA item: prompt, question, choices, and the set of gold (correct) choice indices.

nodes.name: load_truthfulqa
nodes.summary: Loads TruthfulQA from HuggingFace with validation, seeded shuffling, and sample capping, converting rows into FactualitySample objects and skipping rows without usable MC targets.

nodes.name: _extract_mc_targets
nodes.summary: Extracts (choices, gold_indices) from a TruthfulQA row, handling both nested mc1_targets payloads and flat parallel choices/labels lists.

nodes.name: _build_truthfulqa_prompt
nodes.summary: Formats a TruthfulQA question and its lettered choices into the multiple-choice prompt string.

nodes.name: toxicity.py
nodes.summary: Dataset loader for RealToxicityPrompts: loads and shuffles the HF dataset, safely coerces prompt toxicity scores to floats, and emits ToxicitySample records with challenge metadata for the legacy toxicity evaluation.

nodes.name: ToxicitySample
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass for one RealToxicityPrompts item: prompt text, optional prompt toxicity score, and metadata.

nodes.name: load_real_toxicity_prompts
nodes.summary: Loads RealToxicityPrompts from HuggingFace with validation, seeded shuffling, and sample capping, extracting prompt text and toxicity from nested or flat payloads into ToxicitySample objects.

nodes.name: _safe_float
nodes.summary: Coerces a value to float, returning None for missing or unparseable inputs instead of raising.

nodes.name: bias_eval.py
nodes.summary: Legacy evaluator for social-bias multiple-choice QA: parses letter/digit answers from model responses, scores accuracy and stereotyped-answer rates, and summarizes results with per-axis breakdowns.

nodes.name: BiasEvalConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass config for bias evaluation (answer-letter regex, bootstrap resamples) with a from_dict constructor applying defaults.

nodes.name: BiasEvaluator
nodes.summary: Scores batches of MC responses against BiasSample gold answers (accuracy, unparsed rate, stereotyped-choice rate) and summarizes results including a per-bias-axis breakdown with prediction/confusion counts.

nodes.name: factuality_eval.py
nodes.summary: Legacy evaluator for TruthfulQA-style multiple-choice factuality: parses answer letters/digits from responses, scores correctness against gold indices, and optionally rates open-ended answers with an LLM judge.

nodes.name: FactualityEvalConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass config for factuality evaluation (enable_llm_judge flag, judge scale bounds) with a from_dict constructor.

nodes.name: FactualityEvaluator
nodes.summary: Evaluates batches of MC factuality responses for correctness and unparsed rate, optionally invoking an LLM judge generator to score answers on a numeric scale, and summarizes aggregate accuracy.

nodes.name: _parse_answer_index
nodes.summary: Parses a model response into a zero-based choice index by matching an answer letter first, then falling back to a digit, returning None when nothing valid is found.

nodes.name: toxicity_eval.py
nodes.summary: Legacy toxicity evaluator with pluggable scorer backends (Detoxify or a HuggingFace text-classification pipeline): scores generated continuations for toxicity probability, thresholds them, and summarizes toxicity rate with bootstrap confidence intervals.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Protocol class (ToxicityScorer) for structural typing of scorer backends instead of an ABC.

nodes.name: ToxicityScorer
nodes.summary: Protocol defining the scorer interface: score_batch maps a list of texts to toxicity probabilities in [0, 1].

nodes.name: ToxicityEvalConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass config for toxicity evaluation (threshold, classifier type/model/batch size, bootstrap resamples) with a from_dict constructor applying defaults.

nodes.name: DetoxifyScorer
nodes.summary: Scorer backend wrapping the Detoxify library, raising a clear ImportError when the optional dependency is missing and extracting the toxicity key from prediction outputs.

nodes.name: HFClassifierScorer
nodes.summary: Scorer backend wrapping a HuggingFace text-classification pipeline, batching inputs and converting heterogeneous pipeline outputs into toxicity probabilities.

nodes.name: _extract_toxicity_probability
nodes.summary: Normalizes a HF pipeline output (dict or list of label/score dicts) into a single toxicity probability, preferring toxic-labelled entries and falling back to the max score.

nodes.name: _score_from_label_dict
nodes.summary: Converts a single label/score dict to a toxicity probability, inverting the score when the label indicates non-toxic.

nodes.name: ToxicityEvaluator
nodes.summary: Orchestrates toxicity scoring: builds the configured scorer backend, evaluates batches of responses against the toxicity threshold, and summarizes toxicity rate with bootstrap confidence intervals and score statistics.

nodes.name: run_benchmark.py
nodes.summary: CLI orchestrator for the legacy ethical benchmark: parses arguments, validates the YAML config, loads each model, streams dataset samples through batched generation and the task-specific evaluator, and persists raw JSONL, summary JSON/CSV, and radar-chart CSV outputs with resume support.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines and parses the CLI arguments for a benchmark run (config path, model/task selection, device, sample cap, output root, resume, logging).

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging with a standard format at the requested level.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the YAML configuration file into a raw dict for schema validation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level run loop: validates the config, seeds RNGs, then for each selected model and task loads the model, samples, and evaluator, runs the generation loop, and persists results.

nodes.name: _persist_results
nodes.summary: Writes the evaluator summary plus run metadata (model spec, device, decoding config, seed) to summary JSON, appends the flattened summary CSV row, and exports radar-chart dimensions CSV.

nodes.name: build_output_paths
nodes.summary: Constructs the per-(task, model) output file paths (raw JSONL, summary JSON, summary CSV, radar CSV), sanitizing slashes in the model alias.

nodes.name: load_samples
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the task-appropriate dataset loader (RealToxicityPrompts, BBQ, or TruthfulQA) with the configured dataset name, split, sample cap, and seed.

nodes.name: build_evaluator
nodes.summary: Factory dispatching on task name to construct the ToxicityEvaluator, BiasEvaluator, or FactualityEvaluator from the task's evaluation config.

nodes.name: run_generation_loop
nodes.summary: Streams samples in batches through the text generator and evaluator, appending per-prompt records to raw JSONL and skipping already-processed prompt IDs when resuming.

nodes.name: batched
nodes.summary: Yields successive fixed-size chunks from a sequence, with the final chunk holding any remainder.

nodes.name: build_radar_dimensions
nodes.summary: Maps task metrics into named, clamped [0, 1] radar-chart dimensions per task type (toxicity, bias, factuality), returning empty for unknown tasks.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures: canned ToxicitySample/BiasSample/FactualitySample lists, a default legacy benchmark config dict, and a quantization-study config dict used across the test suite.

nodes.name: toxicity_samples
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture returning a small list of ToxicitySample objects for evaluator tests.

nodes.name: bias_samples
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture returning BiasSample objects with known gold and stereotyped indices for bias evaluator tests.

nodes.name: factuality_samples
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture returning FactualitySample objects with known gold indices for factuality evaluator tests.

nodes.name: default_config
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture providing a complete legacy benchmark config dict (models, decoding, toxicity/bias/factuality tasks) for schema and pipeline tests.

nodes.name: quant_config_dict
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture providing a quantization-study config dict (model pairs with baseline/4-bit members, benchmarks) used by the quant config-schema tests.

nodes.name: test_datasets.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the legacy dataset loaders: sample dataclass creation/immutability, choice and gold-index parsing helpers, MC target extraction, and loader input validation errors.

nodes.name: TestSafeFloat
nodes.summary: Tests _safe_float coercion across None, floats, ints, numeric strings, and invalid strings.

nodes.name: TestToxicitySample
nodes.summary: Tests ToxicitySample construction and frozen-dataclass immutability.

nodes.name: TestExtractChoices
nodes.summary: Tests _extract_choices across ans-key rows, choices lists, answers lists, and empty rows.

nodes.name: TestParseChoiceIndex
nodes.summary: Tests _parse_choice_index for valid/out-of-range integers, letter strings, None, and bool inputs.

nodes.name: TestExtractMCTargets
nodes.summary: Tests _extract_mc_targets for mc1_targets payloads, flat choices/labels rows, and empty rows.

nodes.name: TestFactualitySample
nodes.summary: Tests FactualitySample construction and immutability.

nodes.name: TestLoaderValidation
nodes.summary: Tests that all three dataset loaders raise ValueError on negative sample counts, empty dataset names, and empty splits.

nodes.name: test_evaluators.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the legacy evaluators: answer-index parsing for bias and factuality, toxicity probability extraction from classifier outputs, and end-to-end evaluate/summarize behavior of BiasEvaluator and FactualityEvaluator.

nodes.name: TestBiasAnswerParsing
nodes.summary: Tests BiasEvaluator._parse_answer_index for single letters, letters in sentences, digits, gibberish, out-of-range, and empty responses.

nodes.name: TestFactualityAnswerParsing
nodes.summary: Tests the factuality _parse_answer_index helper for letters, letters with periods, digit fallback, and empty responses.

nodes.name: TestExtractToxicityProbability
nodes.summary: Tests _extract_toxicity_probability for toxic/non-toxic label dicts, list outputs, missing toxic labels, and unexpected types.

nodes.name: TestBiasEvaluator
nodes.summary: Tests BiasEvaluator.evaluate_batch record output and summarize accuracy computation, including the empty-records case.

nodes.name: TestFactualityEvaluator
nodes.summary: Tests FactualityEvaluator.evaluate_batch and summarize accuracy computation, including the empty-records case.

nodes.name: test_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for legacy pipeline helper functions: batching, output-path construction with alias sanitization, and radar-dimension building with clamping.

nodes.name: TestBatched
nodes.summary: Tests the batched chunking helper for exact division, remainders, empty input, single elements, and oversized batch sizes.

nodes.name: TestBuildOutputPaths
nodes.summary: Tests that output paths embed task and model alias and that slashes in aliases are sanitized.

nodes.name: TestBuildRadarDimensions
nodes.summary: Tests radar-dimension construction per task type, the unknown-task empty case, and value clamping into [0, 1].

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the metrics I/O and bootstrap-CI helpers from aggregate.py for use across the pipeline and analysis layers.

nodes.name: aggregate.py
nodes.summary: Core metrics I/O utilities: JSONL append/read (with gzip support), JSON writing, summary CSV upsert, nested-dict flattening for CSV, radar CSV export, and a bootstrap confidence-interval estimator used by benchmark aggregation.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-stdlib + NumPy module with no project-internal imports, making it the leaf dependency of the pipeline layer.

nodes.name: compute_bootstrap_ci
nodes.summary: Computes a seeded bootstrap confidence interval (mean plus lower/upper quantiles) over an array of metric values, returning None for empty input.

nodes.name: append_jsonl
nodes.summary: Appends record dicts to a JSONL file, creating parent directories and transparently handling .gz-compressed paths.

nodes.name: read_jsonl
nodes.summary: Reads a JSONL (optionally gzipped) file into a list of dicts, returning an empty list when the file does not exist; used by resume logic to recover processed prompts.

nodes.name: write_json
nodes.summary: Writes a dict to a JSON file, creating parent directories as needed; used for per-run summary.json artifacts.

nodes.name: append_summary_csv
nodes.summary: Upserts a flattened summary row into the cross-run CSV, replacing an existing row with the same run identity so resumed runs do not duplicate entries.

nodes.name: flatten_for_csv
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens nested dicts into dot/underscore-prefixed scalar keys so summary metrics can be written as flat CSV columns.

nodes.name: export_radar_csv
nodes.summary: Writes a two-column dimension/score CSV from a summary dict for radar-chart visualization.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel exposing the single-run (run_quant_benchmark) and matrix (run_quant_matrix) orchestration modules of the quantization pipeline.

nodes.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Single-run orchestrator: loads a (possibly NF4/INT8-quantized) HF model per the validated config, batches benchmark prompts through the generator, scores each response via the benchmark plugin, and persists raw.jsonl, summary.json, and summary CSV artifacts with resume support based on processed prompt IDs.
nodes.languageNotes: Model-loading classes (HFModelLoader, TextGenerator, DecodingConfig) are resolved lazily via get_model_stack() so tests can monkeypatch the stack without importing torch/transformers.

nodes.name: get_model_stack
nodes.summary: Lazily imports and returns the model-loading stack (loader, generator, spec, decoding config, seed function) so heavy ML dependencies load only when actually running a benchmark.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI for a single benchmark run: config path, model alias, benchmark name, device, sample/batch overrides, seed, and resume/force-restart flags.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging at the requested level with a consistent timestamped format for pipeline runs.

nodes.name: normalize_model_key
nodes.summary: Normalizes a model alias into a lowercase, underscore-safe key used for output directory names.

nodes.name: _resolved_dtype_name
nodes.summary: Resolves a config dtype string (including 'auto') to the concrete dtype name given the runtime device (float16 on CUDA, float32 on CPU) for recording in summary metadata.

nodes.name: build_run_paths
nodes.summary: Constructs the canonical output paths (raw.jsonl, summary.json, summary CSV) for a model-alias/benchmark run under the results directory.

nodes.name: validate_record_schema
nodes.summary: Validates that a per-prompt result record carries the full audit schema (pair_id, quantized flag, seed, generation config, timestamp, etc.), raising ValueError on missing or malformed fields.

nodes.name: get_processed_prompt_ids
nodes.summary: Reads an existing raw.jsonl and returns the set of prompt IDs already processed, enabling interrupted runs to resume without re-generating.

nodes.name: prepare_remaining_items
nodes.summary: Filters the benchmark item list down to items whose prompt IDs have not yet been processed, preserving order for deterministic resumes.

nodes.name: batched
nodes.summary: Yields fixed-size batches from the item list for batched generation, guarding against non-positive batch sizes.

nodes.name: execute_quant_benchmark_loaded
nodes.summary: Core run loop given an already-loaded generator: builds the benchmark plugin, resolves resume state, batches prompts through generation, scores and schema-validates each record, appends raw.jsonl, aggregates metrics, and writes summary.json plus the flattened summary CSV row.

nodes.name: execute_quant_benchmark
nodes.summary: Full single-run entry: seeds all RNGs, builds the ModelSpec from the config entry (including on-the-fly quantization flags), loads model and tokenizer via HFModelLoader, constructs the TextGenerator, then delegates to execute_quant_benchmark_loaded.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, sets up logging, loads and validates the quantization config, and runs the selected model/benchmark combination.

nodes.name: run_quant_matrix.py
nodes.summary: Matrix orchestrator that runs every configured model across its benchmarks; in reuse mode it loads each model once, runs all its benchmarks sequentially via execute_quant_benchmark_loaded, and releases GPU memory (gc + cuda.empty_cache) in a try/finally between models.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI for matrix runs: config path, model/benchmark filters, device, sample/batch overrides, resume flags, and the model-reuse toggle.

nodes.name: _resolve_models
nodes.summary: Resolves the user's model selection against the configured model aliases, raising KeyError for unknown aliases and defaulting to all models.

nodes.name: _selected_benchmarks_for_model
nodes.summary: Computes the benchmark list for a model by intersecting its configured benchmarks with an optional CLI benchmark filter.

nodes.name: _release_model_memory
nodes.summary: Frees model memory between matrix entries via gc.collect and, when torch/CUDA are present, torch.cuda.empty_cache — accessed defensively through sys.modules so the module works without torch installed.
nodes.languageNotes: Looks torch up via sys.modules.get instead of importing it, keeping the matrix module importable in torch-free test environments.

nodes.name: run_quant_matrix
nodes.summary: Runs the full model-by-benchmark matrix: without reuse it delegates each (model, benchmark) pair to execute_quant_benchmark; with reuse it loads each model once, runs all its benchmarks through execute_quant_benchmark_loaded, and releases memory in a try/finally.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, loads the validated config, resolves model selection, and launches the matrix run.

nodes.name: run_benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Thin repo-root launcher that delegates to the pipeline's single-run benchmark entry point for backwards-compatible invocation.

nodes.name: run_quant_benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_benchmark.main so a single quantized benchmark run can be launched directly (e.g. from sbatch scripts).

nodes.name: run_quant_matrix.py
nodes.summary: Repo-root shim that invokes ethical_benchmark.pipeline.run_quant_matrix.main to launch the full model-by-benchmark matrix (used by SLURM matrix jobs).

nodes.name: test_matrix_reuse.py
nodes.summary: Tests the matrix runner's model-reuse logic with monkeypatched dummy loader/generator stacks: reuse mode must load each model exactly once across its benchmarks, while no-reuse mode must delegate to the single-run entry per (model, benchmark).

nodes.name: _DummyLoader
nodes.summary: Test double for HFModelLoader that records how many times load() is called per model, letting the reuse test assert one load per model.

nodes.name: test_matrix_reuse_loads_once_per_model
nodes.summary: Asserts that with reuse_loaded_model enabled, run_quant_matrix loads each model exactly once and executes every configured benchmark through the loaded-model path.

nodes.name: test_matrix_no_reuse_calls_single_runner
nodes.summary: Asserts that with reuse disabled, run_quant_matrix falls back to calling the single-run execute_quant_benchmark once per (model, benchmark) combination.

nodes.name: test_metrics_and_config.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the metrics I/O layer (bootstrap CI, CSV flattening, JSONL round-trips, JSON writing, summary-CSV upsert, radar CSV) plus config validation failure modes (missing models, invalid dtype, negative token counts, out-of-range temperature).

nodes.name: TestBootstrapCI
nodes.summary: Verifies compute_bootstrap_ci behavior on normal arrays, empty input (returns None), configurable confidence levels, and single-value degenerate cases.

nodes.name: TestFlattenForCSV
nodes.summary: Verifies flatten_for_csv on flat, nested, deeply nested, and mixed-type dicts.

nodes.name: TestJSONLRoundTrip
nodes.summary: Round-trip tests for append_jsonl/read_jsonl covering write-then-read, append preserving existing records, and reading nonexistent files.

nodes.name: TestWriteJSON
nodes.summary: Verifies write_json persists a dict that reads back identically.

nodes.name: TestSummaryCSV
nodes.summary: Verifies append_summary_csv creates headers, appends distinct rows, and upserts (replaces) the row for the same run identity on resume rather than duplicating it.

nodes.name: TestRadarCSV
nodes.summary: Verifies export_radar_csv writes the expected header and one row per summary dimension.

nodes.name: TestConfigValidation
nodes.summary: Negative-path tests for config validation: a valid config passes while missing models, invalid dtype, negative max_new_tokens, and out-of-range temperature all raise.

nodes.name: test_quant_pipeline_utils.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the single-run pipeline's helper functions: record schema validation (accept/reject), resume filtering via prepare_remaining_items, and processed-prompt-ID recovery from raw.jsonl.

nodes.name: _valid_record
nodes.summary: Factory returning a fully-populated per-prompt record dict that satisfies the pipeline's audit schema, used as the baseline fixture for validation tests.

nodes.name: test_quant_smoke.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke tests of the single-run pipeline using dummy model/plugin doubles: each benchmark type runs through execute_quant_benchmark producing raw.jsonl and summary.json, and identical seeds yield identical prompt ordering across runs.

nodes.name: _DummyPlugin
nodes.summary: Stub BenchmarkPlugin that emits seeded, shuffled BenchmarkItems and computes benchmark-specific aggregate metrics (ASR, over-refusal, accuracy) so the pipeline can be exercised without datasets or models.

nodes.name: test_smoke_each_benchmark
nodes.summary: Runs execute_quant_benchmark for each configured benchmark with the dummy stack and asserts the raw.jsonl and summary.json artifacts are produced at the expected paths.

nodes.name: test_reproducible_order_same_seed
nodes.summary: Runs the same benchmark twice with the same seed into separate output dirs and asserts the per-prompt record order in raw.jsonl is identical, proving deterministic item ordering.

nodes.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
nodes.summary: Repo-root convenience entrypoint that simply re-exports and runs main() from ethical_benchmark.analysis.compare_quant_pairs, so the pairwise analysis can be invoked as a top-level script.

nodes.name: compare_quant_pairs.py
nodes.summary: Core pairwise quantization analysis module: computes baseline-vs-4bit absolute/relative deltas per benchmark, paired bootstrap CIs and McNemar exact tests, and assigns interpretation labels (alignment_degradation, capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, robust_preservation, etc.). Its statistical primitives are reused by every downstream analysis script (judge agreement, multiple comparisons, precision sweep).
nodes.languageNotes: Single source of truth for McNemar/bootstrap statistics; downstream scripts import these primitives rather than recomputing, so all reported numbers stay identical across artifacts.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses CLI arguments for the analysis run: config path, results directory, output directory, and log level.

nodes.name: setup_logging
nodes.summary: Configures root logging with a standard format at the requested level.

nodes.name: _read_json
nodes.summary: Safely reads a JSON file, returning None when the path does not exist instead of raising.

nodes.name: _select_pair_members
nodes.summary: Selects the baseline (fp16) and NF4-quantized members of a pair_id from the config, deliberately excluding INT8 variants so the main NF4 study never silently compares against the wrong precision.

nodes.name: _extract_metric
nodes.summary: Pulls a named primary metric (e.g. attack_success_rate, accuracy) out of a run summary dict, coercing to float and tolerating missing keys.

nodes.name: compute_relative_delta
nodes.summary: Computes the relative delta from a baseline value and an absolute delta, guarding against division by a zero baseline.

nodes.name: classify_pair_change
nodes.summary: Assigns the project's interpretation label to a pair from its harm, over-refusal, and capability deltas under tolerance thresholds -- distinguishing true alignment shifts from capability collapse masquerading as safety. This is the project's central classification rule, reused by the dashboard and judge scripts.

nodes.name: label_evidence_status
nodes.summary: Attaches a two-layer evidence status to an interpretation label based on which of the underlying deltas (harm, capability, over-refusal) are statistically significant, so descriptive labels are never over-claimed as significant findings.

nodes.name: paired_binary_confusion
nodes.summary: Builds the 2x2 concordant/discordant counts (b, c) over the shared prompt IDs of baseline and quantized per-prompt binary outcomes, the input to McNemar's test.

nodes.name: mcnemar_exact_test
nodes.summary: Exact two-sided McNemar test: computes the binomial p-value from discordant counts b and c using math.comb, with no scipy dependency.

nodes.name: _extract_binary_outcome
nodes.summary: Maps benchmark-specific score fields (harmful flag for HarmBench, refusal flag for XSTest, correctness for MMLU/ARC) to a single binary outcome for paired statistics.

nodes.name: _read_per_prompt_outcomes
nodes.summary: Streams a raw/scores JSONL file and returns a prompt_id-to-binary-outcome map for one benchmark run, skipping malformed lines.

nodes.name: _prefer_corrected_score_paths
nodes.summary: Swaps in the post-hoc corrected v2 sidecar paths (summary.v2.json / scores.v2.jsonl) when they exist, so the analysis prefers rescored artifacts over immutable runtime originals.

nodes.name: compute_paired_bootstrap_ci
nodes.summary: Seeded paired-bootstrap resampling over shared prompt IDs producing confidence intervals for the baseline rate, quantized rate, and their delta; significance is read off whether the delta CI excludes zero.

nodes.name: build_pairwise_report
nodes.summary: Assembles the per-pair, per-benchmark delta rows: resolves run paths, prefers corrected sidecars, extracts primary metrics, and augments each row with paired bootstrap CIs and significance flags.

nodes.name: summarize_pair_labels
nodes.summary: Groups pairwise delta rows by pair_id and derives each pair's interpretation label plus its two-layer evidence status from the harm/over-refusal/capability cells.

nodes.name: compute_scale_sensitivity
nodes.summary: Compares delta magnitudes between the smaller and larger Qwen pairs to test whether quantization effects shrink or grow with model scale.

nodes.name: compute_cross_family_consistency
nodes.summary: Measures sign agreement of per-benchmark deltas across model families (Qwen, Llama, Mistral, Phi), producing pairwise family comparisons and an overall consistency verdict per metric.

nodes.name: write_csv
nodes.summary: Writes a list of dict rows to CSV with a union header, creating parent directories and handling the empty-rows case.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI orchestrator: loads the quantization config, builds the pairwise report, derives labels, computes scale sensitivity and cross-family consistency, then writes pairwise_deltas, pair_interpretations, and the analysis summary as JSON/CSV under results/analysis.

nodes.name: judge_agreement.py
nodes.summary: GPU-free comparison of the primary HarmBench classifier judge against the v2 regex scorer: per-model agreement %, Cohen's kappa and 2x2 confusion, per-pair delta-ASR under both scorers, and whether the interpretation label would flip under the judge. Writes results/analysis/judge_agreement.{json,csv} from redacted (IDs + booleans) sidecars only.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads only redacted judge sidecars (prompt IDs + booleans) -- no prompt or response text touches this script, preserving the project's redaction policy.

nodes.name: _load_jsonl_flag
nodes.summary: Loads a prompt_id-to-boolean map from a JSONL sidecar, reading the requested flag key from either the top level or nested score_fields.

nodes.name: _load_judge_outcomes
nodes.summary: Reads the redacted scores.judge.<name>.jsonl sidecar for a model directory and returns prompt_id-to-judge_harmful booleans.

nodes.name: _load_v2_harmful
nodes.summary: Reads the v2 regex scorer sidecar (scores.v2.jsonl) and returns prompt_id-to-is_harmful booleans for comparison against the judge.

nodes.name: _cohens_kappa
nodes.summary: Computes Cohen's kappa between two aligned boolean sequences, handling the degenerate all-agree case.

nodes.name: _confusion
nodes.summary: Builds the 2x2 confusion of v2-harmful vs judge-harmful over shared prompt IDs, returning agreement rate, kappa, and each scorer's positive counts.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the judge-vs-regex study: per-model confusion/kappa/ASR, per-pair judge delta-ASR with McNemar and bootstrap CIs, re-derives interpretation labels with the judge ASR substituted for v2 ASR, writes judge_agreement.{json,csv}, and prints a console report.

nodes.name: judge_pairwise_agreement.py
nodes.summary: Judge-vs-judge robustness check (T22/W3): cross-checks the primary HarmBench classifier against a second independent judge (GPT-4o api_judge) on agreement, kappa, and per-pair delta-ASR sign/significance concordance, reusing the same McNemar/bootstrap apparatus as the primary analysis. Writes results/analysis/judge_pairwise_agreement.{json,csv}.

nodes.name: load_judge_outcomes
nodes.summary: Loads a judge's redacted per-prompt harmful booleans from scores.judge.<name>.jsonl for one model directory.

nodes.name: agreement_and_kappa
nodes.summary: Computes agreement rate and Cohen's kappa between two judges over their shared prompt set, plus each judge's positive count.

nodes.name: _pair_delta
nodes.summary: Computes a judge's delta-ASR for one pair with the shared McNemar exact test and paired-bootstrap CI, so the second judge's statistics are identical in form to the primary analysis.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the two-judge comparison across all model aliases and pairs, reporting per-model agreement and per-pair delta-ASR concordance (sign and significance), then writes judge_pairwise_agreement.{json,csv}.

nodes.name: multiple_comparisons.py
nodes.summary: Statistical-rigour layer for the pairwise study: McNemar exact tests on paired judge outcomes, paired bootstrap CIs, Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction across all pair-by-benchmark contrasts, plus MDE/power notes; writes analysis/multiple_comparisons.json consumed by the dashboard and report.

nodes.name: _paired_outcomes
nodes.summary: Aligns baseline and quantized per-prompt judge outcomes by prompt_id into paired binary vectors.

nodes.name: mde_mcnemar
nodes.summary: Computes the minimum detectable effect for a McNemar test at the observed discordant-pair count.

nodes.name: power_mcnemar
nodes.summary: Estimates statistical power of the McNemar contrast given discordant counts and alpha.

nodes.name: benjamini_hochberg
nodes.summary: Applies BH-FDR correction across all pairwise contrasts and flags which survive.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Builds every pair-by-benchmark contrast from judge/v2 sidecars and emits multiple_comparisons.json with tests, CIs, and FDR verdicts.

nodes.name: precision_sweep_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Builds the fp16 -> INT8 -> NF4 precision-sweep table per pair and benchmark: metric trend classification (graded/cliff/non-monotonic) and INT8 McNemar significance; feeds the report's precision-point section.

nodes.name: _trend
nodes.summary: Classifies the fp16->INT8->NF4 metric trajectory as graded, cliff-at-NF4, or non-monotonic.

nodes.name: _int8_mcnemar
nodes.summary: Runs McNemar on paired judge outcomes between baseline and INT8 members.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Assembles the precision sweep across pairs and writes JSON + CSV outputs.

nodes.name: test_precision_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Tests for precision_sweep_analysis: trend classification, graceful handling of pending INT8 runs, v2-sidecar preference, and reproduction of the documented Llama INT8 McNemar result.

nodes.name: test_quant_analysis.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the pairwise delta/interpretation engine: classify_pair_change labels, two-layer evidence status, McNemar and bootstrap helpers, v2-sidecar preference, and NF4/INT8 member selection guards.

nodes.name: refusal_margin.py
nodes.summary: Token-level refusal-margin diagnostics: log-probability margin between refusal and compliance first-token sets, decision entropy, and top1-top2 gap, used to test whether quantization shifts the refusal decision boundary mechanistically.

nodes.name: log_prob_of_set
nodes.summary: Log-sum-exp probability mass assigned to a set of token ids from first-token logits.

nodes.name: refusal_margin
nodes.summary: Signed log-prob margin between refusal-token and compliance-token sets for one generation step.

nodes.name: decision_entropy
nodes.summary: Entropy of the first-token distribution as a decision-confidence diagnostic.

nodes.name: top1_top2_gap
nodes.summary: Probability gap between the top-1 and top-2 first tokens.

nodes.name: build_token_sets
nodes.summary: Builds refusal vs compliance first-token id sets from a tokenizer, handling leading-space variants and dropping ambiguous tokens.

nodes.name: generation.py
nodes.summary: Batched text generation over loaded HF models: DecodingConfig (greedy, temperature 0.0 defaults), TextGenerator that applies the native chat template with enable_thinking=False and records prompt provenance, plus global seeding for determinism.

nodes.name: set_global_seed
nodes.summary: Seeds Python, NumPy, and Torch (incl. CUDA) for deterministic runs.

nodes.name: DecodingConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen decoding parameters (max_new_tokens, temperature, sampling flags) built from config dicts.

nodes.name: TextGenerator
nodes.summary: Batches prompts through the model: chat-template formatting (enable_thinking=False), generation, and post-processing; flags whether each prompt was genuinely templated.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: HF model + tokenizer loading with dtype/device resolution and on-the-fly quantization: builds BitsAndBytesConfig for NF4 4-bit or LLM.int8, verifies quantization actually engaged after load (hard fail otherwise), and defines the ModelSpec contract.

nodes.name: _build_bnb_4bit_config
nodes.summary: Constructs the NF4 BitsAndBytesConfig with compute dtype following the resolved model dtype.

nodes.name: _build_bnb_8bit_config
nodes.summary: Constructs the LLM.int8 BitsAndBytesConfig for the INT8 precision point.

nodes.name: _quantization_active
nodes.summary: Detects whether a loaded model is actually quantized (is_loaded_in_4bit/8bit, hf_quantizer signals).

nodes.name: _require_quantization_engaged
nodes.summary: Raises if a member configured as quantized silently loaded in fp16 — guards against methodology-invalidating fallbacks.

nodes.name: build_model_spec
nodes.summary: Resolves a config model alias into a ModelSpec (model_id, quantized flag, quant_method, dtype, attn_implementation).

nodes.name: ModelSpec
nodes.summary: Immutable specification of one model member (alias, model_id, quantization method, dtype, device, attention implementation).

nodes.name: HFModelLoader
nodes.summary: Loads model+tokenizer from the spec with device/dtype resolution and optional BitsAndBytes quantization.

nodes.name: capture_refusal_margin.py
nodes.summary: TC1 capture script that re-runs HarmBench prompts through each model recording first-token logits and refusal-margin diagnostics as redacted sidecars for the mechanistic refusal analysis.

nodes.name: _sequence_margins

tour.title: What this project is
tour.description: Start with the README for the research question — do safety changes in 4-bit quantized models reflect true alignment shifts or capability degradation? — and CLAUDE.md for how the repo governs itself (PROJECT_LOG as source of truth, claim-lock discipline).

tour.title: The experiment, declared as config
tour.description: Every run starts from YAML validated by a Pydantic schema: five model pairs sharing a model_id (baseline vs 4-bit), three benchmarks, greedy decoding, seed 42. tc1_512.yaml is the PRIMARY study config (D41).

tour.title: Loading models — where quantization happens
tour.description: HFModelLoader resolves dtype/device and applies BitsAndBytes NF4 (or INT8) on the fly at load; a guard hard-fails if quantization silently didn't engage. TextGenerator then does deterministic chat-templated batch generation.

tour.title: The three benchmark lenses
tour.description: All plugins implement one ABC: HarmBench measures harmful compliance (ASR), XSTest measures over-refusal on benign prompts, MMLU anchors capability. Each scores its own responses.

tour.title: Running: single run to full matrix
tour.description: run_quant_benchmark orchestrates one model-benchmark run (seed, load, generate, score, write raw.jsonl + summary.json); run_quant_matrix loads each model once for all its benchmarks and releases GPU memory between models.

tour.title: Judge scoring — the primary ASR
tour.description: The official HarmBench 13B classifier is the primary HarmBench scorer (D16): it re-scores raw outputs into redacted judge sidecars (IDs + booleans only), never touching immutable raw artifacts. Agreement stats quantify how much the regex proxy over-counts.

tour.title: From numbers to claims
tour.description: Pairwise deltas get interpretation labels (alignment_degradation vs capability_collapse_masquerading_as_safety, etc.); multiple_comparisons applies McNemar + BH-FDR across all contrasts; the genlen study showed the 128-token headline was a truncation artefact — at 512 no significant ASR increase survives anywhere.

tour.title: Running on the TC1 cluster
tour.description: Jobs are generated as per-model sbatch scripts (offline env, bootstrap block preserved), submitted under strict QoS limits; prefetch_tc1 caches models/datasets on the head node so compute nodes run fully offline.

tour.title: Exploring results — the dashboard
tour.description: The Streamlit app presents judge-primary results (rebuilt from multiple_comparisons.json, NOT the v2-proxy interpretations), lets you add a model pair via a validated form, and launches runs; data.py is the tested, Streamlit-free data layer.

tour.title: The deliverables — report & thesis, claim-locked
tour.description: The docx report/thesis are generated from JS builders (never hand-edited); verify_report_claims machine-checks ~55 numbers and citations against primary artifacts on every pytest run, and number_bible_512 prints the single source for every headline value.

tour.title: The agent harness — the repo that maintains itself
tour.description: A policy file drives machine checks: immutable-artifact manifests, CLAUDE/AGENTS sync, PROJECT_LOG discipline, stale-claim and redaction scans, report freshness. agent_check is the gate; renderers produce handoff/dashboard/checklist docs.

tour.title: How it's all verified
tour.description: The test suite proves each layer: end-to-end pipeline smoke, delta/interpretation math, judge redaction and idempotency, INT8 scoping guards, dashboard data invariants, and harness self-evaluation on fixture repos.
